node.text:
Sometimes it’s the quiet moments that make the day. Hope you have a great one. 💕
 
#MicrosoftLife #TheArtOfNoticing

Description: a montage of short clips capturing leaves in the sky, a campus water feature, working outside with nature in the background, an espresso shot glass being filled, steaming milk, shadows and light on a workspace, and petting a cat while working from home.

node.text: The attention to detail is a 10/10. 🎁🎁
now Bing me some figgy pudding 🎶
it's pic #4 for me 😩!
This year's #WindowsUglySweater is iconic. ✨ Grab yours at the link in bio.

We asked Image Creator by Designer to create an image of the Chiang Mai Lantern Festival that takes place in northern Thailand, especially in Chiang Mai, during the full moon night of the 12th month of the Thai lunar calendar (this year, November 27-28). Celebrate with us by dropping a 🌕 in our replies.

node.text:
We’re at #AfroTech23! Thanks to Anthony Kerr (Microsoft Sr. AI & Search PMM, Blacks at Microsoft Chair) for sharing a peek into his week.
 
Shout-out to co-hosts: @andrewcberkley (Microsoft Responsible AI PMM) and @sineadbovell (WAYE Founder)

#MicrosoftLife #PeopleOfMicrosoft #AfroTech

node.text: Dia de los Muertos is a time when families and friends come together to remember and honor the lives of loved ones who have passed away. Thanks for helping us celebrate this year, @Bing.
Transparency is key. Read our full D&I report at the link in bio.
Unleash your creativity with AI and turn your pumpkins into masterpieces 👻 try now at the link in our bio.

node.text: Happy Halloween to those who celebrate. 🎃

So excited to be invited by @microsoft to speak at @afro.tech ! We’ll be showcasing how generative AI is changing the game for productivity and creativity. Join me at Learning Lab Stage 2 this Thursday (Nov 2) at 9am CT! See you there! #MicrosoftPartner #AfroTech

node.text:
WAIT FOR IT... There are two types of people. Which one are you?
 
You do you. There are lots of ways to get stuff done, and we’re here for it however you do.
 
#MicrosoftLife #Microsoft

Description: A desktop screen with 3 icons and calm piano music switches to a desktop full of icons with fast rock music.

node.text: Fall ASMR is the coziest ASMR.🕯️😌
Well that’s excellent.

Early in life, Telmo Sampaio learned the strength of embracing differences. Today this Principal Service Engineering Manager is empowering engineers to bring their diverse backgrounds, values, and ideas to the workplace.  
 
Discover more stories like Telmo’s by visiting the link in our bio.

#InclusionIsInnovation #PeopleOfMicrosoft #MicrosoftLife #DiversityAndInclusion

node.text:
We’re working with @microsoft to demonstrate how fashion creators can innovate their design process with text-to-image inspiration that allows them to explore outfit concepts and combinations before putting needle to fabric. If you can imagine it, Microsoft AI can help you model it. ⁠
⁠
📽️ @microsoft

node.text:
Sandra, originally from Mexico, lives in Estonia. Her experience embracing multiple cultures prepared her to face challenges when managing orientation for employees from all over the world. Illustrated story by artist Niege Borges. 

Explore more by visiting the link in our bio.  
 
#InclusionIsInnovation #PeopleOfMicrosoft #MicrosoftLife #DiversityAndInclusion

node.text:
☕ or 🍵?
Cheers to a great week ahead! 

#MicrosoftLife 

Description: coffee and tea being prepared simultaneously, ending with the two mugs on a table picked up and tapped.

node.text: Embark on your own epic journey through the stars on September 6. #Starfield

Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has steered the company to pole position in the AI race. ⁠
⁠
Nadella struck a landmark partnership with ChatGPT creator OpenAI, which—in return for a reported $13 billion investment—gives the software giant first dibs at the startup’s current and upcoming technologies. As the results have started showing up in new versions of @Microsoft products—from GitHub to Bing to Excel to Azure—they’ve greatly boosted the company’s standing in relation to peers such as Amazon and Google. For the first time since its 1990s heyday, the company is widely regarded as the pacemaker in technology’s next historic wave of change.⁠
⁠
Microsoft has been reengineering itself into an AI company for some time—quietly but dramatically. “What happened in the last five months,” he says, “was work of the last 10 years.”⁠
⁠
Read more about how Nadella intends to hold on to a lead that’s anything but certain in the September 2023 cover story at the link in our bio.⁠
⁠
[Photo: Ian Allen]
It’s time for a little snack.

node.text: the sweetest way to mend a broken heart with @bing Image Creator

We were excited to join together the @AjaWilsonFoundation and @VegasPBS to support efforts and provide dyslexia focused digital resources like Immersive Reader in Microsoft 365 to students and educators. Dyslexia hasn't stopped @Aja22Wilson and @Jewellloyd, and we hope to foster inclusivity and reduce stigmas around different ways to learn.
 
Read more on Unlocked, link in bio.

node.text:
Unlock the Autumn Winter 23 ‘Symphony’ collection with Digital ID’s. Connected by EON. Powered by Microsoft.

In collaboration with @EON and @Microsoft, AW23 blurs the boundaries between the physical and digital. Scan the Digital IDs to reveal the full story of each garment, from the creative conception and innovative fabrication to exclusive content and interviews.

“We create clothes that are meant to be cherished and stand the test of time. Each piece tells a unique story that draws inspiration from the rich tapestry of art, literature, music and culture. With the help of Eon and Microsoft, we can now share that story with the world. Being environmentally and socially conscious is something that is very important to us, we want people to engage with the history of each garment, how it was made, where it has come from and, when they’re finished enjoying the piece, to be able to give it a new life.”

- Creative Director and Founder, Priya Ahluwalia.

node.text:
What do 3D, embroidery, and #AI-generated nudibranchs have in common? They’re all part of motion designer Rita Louro’s creative world. #Microsoft #Bing 

See more on Unlocked, link in bio.

We asked Twitter users to create an unexpected and adorable animal pairing using Bing Image Creator, and they delivered. Swipe through for all the cuteness. 😍
--
Courtesy of Twitter users MrDKOz, LordWaldimart, Manu3ro and AndHib

node.text: Eating healthy is a priority for me but so is efficiency because y’all know I’m #fakedomestic! @Microsoft’s new AI-powered Bing was the perfect solution to help me find quick and healthy recipes and honestly is a great tool to find answers for many things from planning a meal to planning a vacation… it’s limitless. #sponsored

Ever wonder how Koalas get work done? We asked the @bing Image Creator to help us picture it. 

This is the prompt we used: a group of koalas working in their treehouse office, during a meeting, wearing business attire, wide shot

node.text: For new hairstyles or new adventures, the new @microsoft AI powered Bing is here to help! Download it and give it a try. #sponsored ✨👩🏻‍💻

node.text:
Time to take a break? Snacking at your desk is fine but there’s a better way to break. Studies show that jotting down a few sources of gratitude can lead to more optimism.
 
TIP: Try using a gratitude journal. Create a doc and open it each day before or after lunch to jot down 2-3 things you’re grateful for. You might be surprised by what actions and feelings you get from this practice.

We’re grateful for snack breaks ... away from the desk. What are you grateful for?
 
S/o to Software Engineer Martin G. for providing the talent for this PSA.
 
#PeopleOfMicrosoft #MicrosoftLife #Microsoft #FlexibleWork #CareerTips #Wellbeing

Description: The video opens with Martin pulling a candy bar out of his desk drawer, then pulls an orange from a potted plant, then candy from a suitcase, and then an ear of corn from behind his monitor.

node.text: The Magic Man Experience uses Microsoft AI to transport fans into Jackson Wang’s captivating imagination. ✨ @88rising

What’s a racoon’s favorite email sign-off? 

Happy rummaging, 
🦝 

We used @bing Image Creator to bring our imagination of an emailing racoon to life. Here’s the prompt we used: raccoon at a standing desk with a laptop sending an email to other colleagues in an open office concept, office is creative and bright, no one else in room, wide shot.

Together with @Microsoft, Junk Kouture empowers creatives to design upcycled creations using STEAM. 

Engineered from sanitized surgical wrap waste, "The Nightingale," honors the power of community and the importance of science, medicine, and technology in building a better, more sustainable world. 

#junkkouturexmicrosoft
Everyone on Instagram: hello

node.text:
Make way for our dream — to empower girls on and off the court to become anything they want to be. 

We partner with @microsoft to bring clinics to girls across Atlanta to showcase the ways STEM can come alive in basketball.

#DoItForTheDream

Office party wish list: fun hats, Hawaiian shirts, plenty of coffee. ✅ 

We asked @Bing Image Creator to show us a group of hedgehogs in Hawaiian shirts and party hats in the office, all drinking coffee out of ceramic mugs, bright and colorful office, photo realistic. 

What should we create next? Leave a prompt in the comments and we’ll pick one to try!

node.text: #ad AI needs to be our friend! Check my bio to see the TLDR for @microsoft ft latest Work Trend Index. #MicrosoftPartner #WorkTrendIndex
And we couldn’t be more excited about it.

node.text: Day 1 at #MSBuild

Currently using @Bing Image Creator to create very important things.

Here’s the prompt we used: a group of dogs in the office taking a selfie around the water cooler.

node.text:
Did someone say groovy tunes? 🎶 Headphones on 🎧, volume up 🔊 and press shuffle 🔀 on your @Microsoft Zune.

➡️ The @GuardiansOfTheGalaxy: The Official Mixtape is now streaming at the link in our Story.
➡️ Bring the #GotGVol3 soundtrack home on vinyl from @disneymusicemporium!

node.text:
Time to take a break? Meeting fatigue is real. Taking just 5 minutes can improve your focus and reduce stress. 

TIP: Start the meetings that you organize 5 minutes later, or reserve break time on your calendar at the start of the week. 

S/o to Sr. Program Manager Charles T. for providing the talent for this PSA. 

#PeopleOfMicrosoft #MicrosoftLife #Microsoft #FlexibleWork #CareerTips #Wellbeing

Description: Charles opens his laptop to find a meeting-packed Outlook calendar. He has an idea and a helmet holding two coffee cups with straw to his mouth magically shows up on his head. He grabs a notepad, when his pen falls to the floor, and he’s unable to reach it on the floor without spilling.

node.text: With @JunkKouture we help students explore how STEAM, junk, and creativity can be combined to design creations that promote a better world. This design, titled “Divide and Conquer,” uses recycled paper and cloth to create a look that symbolizes the divisions in society.
*wipes tear* 💙

#MicrosoftPartner #sponsored Microsoft’s third annual Work Trend Index report is out today, capturing insights from a survey of 31,000 people across 31 countries, as well as trillions of anonymous and aggregated signals – from emails, meetings, and chats. This year’s report focuses on the emerging role of AI in the workplace. See link in bio for the full report. 

One of the key findings? We have too much to do, and too little time to do it all. We’re drowning in a deep sea of digital debt: data, emails, meetings, and chats. We can’t tread water fast enough to stay afloat, and it’s costing us time, energy, and innovation. Microsoft’s research finds:
- 64% of employees don’t have enough time and energy to get their work done, and those employees are 3.5x more likely to say they struggle with being innovative.
- The average person spends 57% of their time communicating and only 43% creating (based on time spent in Microsoft 365)
- 68% of people say they don’t have enough focus time, and the #1 productivity disruptor is inefficient meetings

We need a meetings makeover! How can we stop sinking? Block out time for deep thinking. We should think of our time more like cake than confetti (Schulte, 2014)—instead of letting our days be torn to shreds, we should proactively plan and protect blocks for deep, individual work, as well as for “bursty” collaborations that allow us to communicate and respond quickly (Riedl & Woolley, 2018). It all comes down to paying attention to our attention—choosing to spend our time on the things that matter and give us energy.

*****

node.text: Knowledge is armor. Our collaboration with @JunkKouture empowers young people to help create a sustainable future through STEAM and creative upcycling.

With @Microsoft, Junk Kouture empowers creatives to design upcycled, waste-free creations like "Back to the Future," a futuristic coat of armor made of plastic and fishing wire, that reminds us to live sustainably to protect our oceans, planet, and future. 

#junkkouturexmicrosoft

node.text:
The King Center Timeline, developed in partnership with @microsoft, chronicles the story of a girl from the south who dared to believe. Mrs. Coretta Scott King’s legacy of love, courage, and compassion inspires us all. Please share your own reflections using #DearCoretta.​
​
